context: Checkpoint 2026-04-29: pós-CI verde, avançamos a lane de streaming com foco em extensibilidade/segurança. Não habilitamos stream:true nem direct wasi::http. Decisão de longo prazo: streaming LLM deve continuar host-proxied no Tractor para preservar credenciais/política no host. ADR-053 proposta e primeiro contrato WIT `llm-bridge::complete-http-stream` introduzido com implementação buffered placeholder no Tractor.
current_tasks: Commits locais não necessariamente pushed desde origin/develop: 2b91ffe1 fix(agent): keep streaming sink model current; a03d038a docs(agent): note fallback stream sink model; fef70936 docs(agent): clarify streaming transport boundary; 9d801190 docs(agent): propose host-proxied llm streaming; 124adfef test(agent): cover stream sink state; b28a8f65 docs(agent): sketch host streaming contract; a388716e docs(agent): link streaming transport adr; 36b88e37 feat(tractor): add llm stream bridge contract; 3797f13c docs(agent): mark stream bridge contract.

ADR-053 added: specs/ADRs/ADR-053-host-proxied-llm-streaming.md. It rejects direct plugin wasi::http for now because that would move provider credentials/policy into the sandbox. Preferred path: host-proxied `complete-http-stream`.

WIT contract added in packages/tractor/wit/host/refarm-plugin-host.wit (pi-agent wit path is a symlink to this file): stream-response-metadata, stream-response-result, complete-http-stream(..., stream-metadata).

Tractor host implements complete_http_stream as a compatibility placeholder: it reuses buffered llm_complete_http, returns final_body, propagates last_sequence, and reports stored_chunks=0. This establishes the boundary without pretending live streaming exists.
pi-agent stream sink state is now testable under native tests. It preserves last_sequence across fallback model updates and ignores None sequence updates.
ROADMAP streaming section updated: contract exists, but chunked HTTP/SSE delivery through complete-http-stream remains pending; streaming_reader_available() stays false.
blockers: Real streaming still not implemented. complete-http-stream is buffered placeholder only; provider_stream_request_enabled_from_env() remains false because streaming_reader_available() is false.
Next implementation must avoid duplicating provider-specific SSE parsing in Tractor unless we accept host-owned AgentResponse chunking or extract shared parser logic.
Host disk remains constrained but improved: ~12GB free; Rust targets ~4.85GB. Continue clean:light after Rust.
Protected surfaces touched: packages/tractor WIT + host bridge. Keep future Tractor changes small/serialized and validated locally before any push.
next_actions: Before push, if needed: git status --short; git log --oneline origin/develop..HEAD; run targeted local checks already used below. Use GitHub Actions only after local reproduction/validation, and monitor with gh run watch --exit-status.

Next technical slice: design the implementation internals of complete-http-stream. Decide if host parses SSE to AgentResponse nodes directly, writes generic StreamChunk nodes, or exposes a safe guest callback/resource stream. Prefer extensibility and minimal coupling.

If implementing live transport: add tests first around host bridge behavior with fake SSE server; only then set streaming_reader_available() true and route pi-agent provider calls to complete_http_stream.

Potential contract refinement: metadata may need provider-family and prompt_ref/model, but host also needs enough schema info to store chunks without hardcoding too much; consider generic StreamChunk node or shared parser crate.

Keep local validations scoped: cd packages/pi-agent && cargo test --lib streaming_sink --quiet; cargo check --target wasm32-wasip1 --quiet; cd packages/tractor && cargo check --quiet; cargo test --lib --quiet; git diff --check; npm run clean:light --silent.
open_questions: Should live host streaming store AgentResponse nodes directly (fast path, but host couples to agent schema) or generic StreamChunk nodes (more extensible, but client/agent reassembly changes)?
Can Component Model safely support host-to-guest callbacks during imported complete_http_stream, or should we avoid reentrancy?
Should provider-specific SSE text delta parsing be extracted to a shared Rust crate used by pi-agent and Tractor?
How should partial chunks account for tokens/duration before final usage arrives?
Error/cancel semantics after partial chunks remain undecided.
key_decisions_pending: Streaming transport implementation shape inside complete-http-stream.
AgentResponse direct chunks vs generic StreamChunk node.
Shared SSE parser extraction vs host duplication.
